name: Old World Walnut Stuffing
recipeIngredient: 6 slice Rye bread, cut into 1/2 inch cubes
6 slice Pumpernickel bread, cut into 1/2 inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 10 inch skillet over medium heat, melt butter; add celery and onions; cook about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally until tender.
2. In a large bowl combine the vegetables with rye and pumpernickel bread cubes, walnuts, parsley, salt, thyme, and pepper; toss well to mix.
3. In a small bowl, beat together eggs and 1/2 cup water; toss with stuffing.
4. Place lightly into neck and body cavities. Makes about 14 cups, enough to stuff 12 to 14 pound turkey.
5. To toast walnuts: In a dry small skillet over very low heat, toast walnuts for 3 to 5 minutes, stirring frequently until golden brown. Cool before adding to stuffing.
